holidays.name: New Year's Day
holidays.description: Celebration of the start of the Gregorian calendar year.

holidays.name: Makar Sankranti / Pongal
holidays.description: Harvest festival celebrated in various forms across India.

holidays.name: Republic Day
holidays.description: Commemorating the date on which the Constitution of India came into effect.

holidays.name: Guru Nanak Birthday
holidays.description: Birth anniversary of the first Sikh Guru.

holidays.name: Maha Shivratri
holidays.description: Celebration of the marriage of Lord Shiva and Goddess Parvati.

holidays.name: Holi
holidays.description: The festival of colors, marking the arrival of spring.

holidays.name: Eid-ul-Fitr
holidays.description: Marking the end of Ramadan, the Islamic holy month of fasting.

holidays.name: Good Friday
holidays.description: Commemoration of the crucifixion of Jesus Christ.

holidays.name: Ambedkar Jayanti
holidays.description: Birth anniversary of Dr. B.R. Ambedkar, the architect of the Indian Constitution.

holidays.name: Buddha Purnima
holidays.description: Celebration of the birth, enlightenment, and death of Gautama Buddha.

holidays.name: Independence Day
holidays.description: Commemorating India's independence from British rule in 1947.

holidays.name: Eid-e-Milad
holidays.description: Observance of the birth of Prophet Muhammad.

holidays.name: Dussehra
holidays.description: Celebration of the victory of Lord Rama over Ravana.

holidays.name: Diwali
holidays.description: The festival of lights, celebrating the victory of light over darkness.

holidays.name: Guru Nanak Birthday
holidays.description: Birth anniversary of the first Sikh Guru.

holidays.name: Christmas
holidays.description: Celebration of the birth of Jesus Christ.
